data.title: Oceaneering CFO Alan Curtis to retire
data.description:
• Oceaneering International (NYSE:OII) on Friday said Alan Curtis, Senior Vice President & CFO, has announced his decision to retire from his position effective January 1, 2026.
  • Curtis began his career with Oceaneering in 1995 as a Financial Controller and has served in a variety of roles, including most recently as CFO since 2015.
  • Michael Sumruld will join Oceaneering as Senior Vice President of Finance on September 1, 2025, and is expected to later succeed Curtis as CFO upon his retirement.
  • Sumruld most recently served as Senior Vice President and CFO for Parker Drilling Company from October 2017 until its sale to Nabors Industries in March 2025. Prior to his time at Parker Drilling, Sumruld held positions at LyondellBasell Industries and at Baker Hughes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BigBear.ai appoints Sean Ricker as interim CFO
data.description:
• BigBear.ai (NYSE:BBAI) announced on Friday that Sean Ricker has been appointed as interim chief financial officer (CFO), effective immediately, succeeding Julie Peffer as CFO.
  • “Sean has a wealth of experience at BigBear.ai, where he most recently held the role of Chief Accounting Officer, prior to which he was Corporate Controller,” said Kevin McAleenan, Chief Executive Officer.
  • “Julie Peffer will be leaving the organization to pursue other opportunities, following a tenure that began in June 2022.” he concluded.
  • BBAI +0.77% after hours to $3.88.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: iOThree receives Nasdaq notice
data.description:
• iOThree Limited (NASDAQ:IOTR) announced on Friday that it has received a Nasdaq notification, indicating that the company was not in compliance with the $1.00 closing bid price requirement.
  • The Nasdaq notification has no immediate effect on the listing of the company’s ordinary shares, which will continue to trade uninterrupted on Nasdaq under the ticker “IOTR”.
  • The company has been granted a 180-calendar day compliance period, or until December 1, 2025, to regain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement.
  • The company said it intends to monitor the closing bid price of its ordinary shares and may, if appropriate, consider implementing available options to cure the deficiency and regain compliance with the minimum bid price requirement within the compliance period under the Nasdaq Listing Rules.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Captivision faces potential Nasdaq delisting over market value, filing deficiencies
data.description:
• Captivision (NASDAQ:CAPT) received a letter from Nasdaq, stating it failed to regain compliance with the $50M market value requirement by the June 2 deadline.
  • CAPT was previously notified of the deficiency on December 3, 2024, and given 180 days to resolve it.
  • Separately, on May 22, 2025, Nasdaq also flagged noncompliance for failing to file its annual report.
  • Captivision plans to request a hearing before a Nasdaq Hearings Panel, which would stay delisting actions for 15 calendar days from the request date.
  • CAPT will also seek an extended stay through the hearing date and any additional period granted by the panel.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: U.S. and Chinese officials set for trade talks in London on Monday
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump on Friday said three members of his administration would hold trade talks with Chinese officials in the United Kingdom on June 9. The announcement comes a day after he held a "very good phone call" with China's leader Xi Jinping.

"I am pleased to announce that Secretary of the Treasury Scott Bessent, Secretary of Commerce Howard Lutnick, and United States Trade Representative, Ambassador Jamieson Greer, will be meeting in London on Monday, June 9, 2025, with Representatives of China, with reference to the Trade Deal. The meeting should go very well," Trump said on his Truth Social service. 

Trump said his call with Xi on Thursday "resulted in a very positive conclusion" for the world's two largest economies, adding that there "should no longer be any questions respecting the complexity of Rare Earth products."

The President said he had been invited by Xi to visit China, and that he had also "reciprocated."

The upcoming meeting in London will be the second between U.S. and Chinese officials since discussions held in Switzerland in mid-May. Those talks had yielded in a surprise temporary trade truce.

But Trump last Friday reignited trade tensions between the countries after accusing China of violating that truce.           

Wall Street on Friday ended sharply higher after a strong May jobs report boosted sentiment and took away the spotlight from trade developments.
